data.title: Yen continues to soar, USD/JPY under 143.50
data.description:
Pressure on the US dollar is continuing.

EUR, JPY, CHF, GBP, gold all strong during Asia morning trade.

USD/JPY notable, its dropped under 143.50.

There is no fresh news nor catalyst apart from what has already been posted.

Markets are now incorporating the risk to Fed independence into their tariff worries.

USD/JPY update:

EUR, JPY, CHF are all fiat alternatives, to some extent, to the USD as a reserve currency.

If you don;t like fiat, there's gold, that's supported also.

data.title: Japan's Nikkei falls 5% on Wall Street's sharp decline
data.description:
Japan's Nikkei share average slumped 5% in early trade on Friday, tracking Wall Street's sharp losses amid worries over uncertainty about the trade war's potential economic fallout.

As of 0012 GMT, the Nikkei was down 5.1% at 32,855.56. The broader Topix slipped 4.7% to 2,419.32.

All three major U.S. stock indexes suffered steep losses overnight, forfeiting much of the previous session's gains as growing concerns over the escalating Washington-Beijing trade face-off dampened optimism over upbeat economic data and U.S.-Europe trade negotiations.

The Nikkei jumped 9% on Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump declared an immediate 90-day tariff pause for many countries.

On Friday, Uniqlo-brand owner Fast Retailing lost 3.87%. Chip-related stocks Tokyo Electron and Advantest slipped 5% and 7.5%, respectively.

All of the Tokyo Stock Exchange's 33 industry sub-indexes fell, with refiners (.IPETE.T) falling 6.6% to become the worst performer.

data.title: WiseTech Global Target Price Cut 8.2% to A$112.50/Share by Bell Potter>WTC.AU
Shift Uncovers Fraudulent Subsidy Applications at Nursery Subsidiary Infinic
data.description:
Shift has uncovered fraudulent subsidy applications made by its consolidated subsidiary Infinic Inc., following an internal audit supported by outside experts, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The misconduct, which dates back to before Shift acquired Infinic in October 2023, was linked to the subsidiary's former representative director, who has been dismissed.

Shift has reported the issue to local authorities and is setting up stronger internal controls at Infinic.

Shares of the company plunged over 9% in morning trade Friday.

Defendants' statements included, among other things, confidence in Skyworks' ability to expand its mobile business and capitalize on its growth potential by investing in new technologies to diversify its portfolio of offerings.</p><p>On February 5, 2025, after market close, Skyworks announced its financial results for the first quarter of fiscal year 2025 and provided lower-than anticipated revenue guidance for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025. The Company attributed its results and low guidance to a "competitive landscape" that had "intensified" in recent years. Following this news, the price of Skyworks' common stock declined dramatically. From a closing market price of $87.08 per share on February 5, 2025, Skyworks' stock price fell to $65.60 per share on February 6, 2025, a decline of over 24% in the span of just a single day.</p><p><strong>WHAT'S NEXT?</strong> If you suffered a loss in Skyworks stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to <a href="https://pr.report/9bv9" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/pslra-1/skyworks-solutions-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=142371&wire=1</a> to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. <strong>There is no cost or obligation to participate.</strong></p><p><strong>WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY:</strong> Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Japanese Stocks Tumble at the Open
data.description:
By Vicky Ge Huang

Japanese shares followed U.S. stocks lower at the open on Friday.

The Nikkei 225 plummeted 5.4% to start trading in Asia.

The stock index jumped 9.1% Thursday, mirroring Wednesday's historic rally in U.S. stocks after President Trump authorized a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for all trading partners except China.

Other Asian-Pacific markets also fell: South Korea's Kospi lost about 1.5% in early trading, while Australia's S&P/ASX 200 fell 2.4%.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: CRE Logistics REIT Secures Exclusive Negotiation Rights for Atsugi Iiyama Minami Site
data.description:
CRE Logistics REIT has obtained exclusive negotiation rights for the Atsugi Iiyama Minami Factory site in Kanagawa Prefecture from SMFL Mirai Partners, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The rights allow the REIT to engage in sale discussions ahead of third parties until April 9, 2028.

The 23,028.31-square-meter site, held under a leasehold interest, is positioned near major expressways and is considered suitable as a distribution hub for Greater Tokyo and Nagoya.

The lease includes a clause to raise rent every five years based on Japan's consumer price index.

Acquisition terms and timing are undecided. The REIT will conduct due diligence before proceeding and is not obligated to buy the property. No fee was paid for the negotiation rights.

data.title: Netwealth Target Price Raised 2.3% to A$26.40/Share by Bell Potter>NWL.AU
Hearts and Minds Investments Portfolio Falls Nearly 9% in March
data.description:
Hearts and Minds Investments' portfolio declined 8.9% in March, bigger than the MSCI World Index's 5.2% drop in the same month, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The decline occurred as equity markets faced volatility during the month amid uncertainties about US trade policies, the filing said.

The investment company also saw the unsuccessful outcome of Opthea's phase 3 clinical trials for its lead drug candidate, OPT-302. This prompted Hearts & Minds to write down its investment to zero.

Before its trading suspension, Opthea represented around 2.4% of Hearts and Minds Investments' portfolio, per a Tuesday statement.

data.title: Polynovo Target Price Cut 29% to A$2.00/Share by Bell Potter>PNV.AU
Market Chatter: Mineral Resources' CEO Remains Upbeat Amidst Financial Struggles
data.description:
Mineral Resources' Founder and Chief Executive Officer Chris Ellison, remains confident in the company's future despite facing significant challenges, including falling commodity prices, operational setbacks, and a AU$5.8 billion debt load, according to a Thursday report by the Australian.

The company also continues to struggle with an AU$230 million repair bill for its Onslow Iron project's haul road in Western Australia, as well as scrutiny over governance issues related to Ellison, the report said.

"Despite all the noise, this is a robust business. We've undergone multiple global shocks and commodity cycles over the years and this will be no different," Ellison reportedly said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: PYC Therapeutics Target Price Cut 23% to A$3.00/Share by Wilsons>PYC.AU
Imricor Medical Systems Starts Clinical Trial at Amsterdam Medical Center
data.description:
Imricor Medical Systems started the VISABL-VT clinical trial after performing the first-in-human ventricular ablation guided by real-time interventional cardiac magnetic resonance (iCMR) at the Amsterdam University Medical Center, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The trial evaluates the safety and effectiveness of radiofrequency ablation of ventricular tachycardia in ischemic cardiomyopathy using the Vision-MR Ablation Catheter 2.0 in the iCMR environment.

The patient was treated for both right-sided and left-sided premature ventricular complexes, the filing said.

Ventricular ablation is a procedure used to treat ventricular tachycardia, a rapid, abnormal heartbeat.

data.title: Maas Resumed at Outperform by Macquarie >MGH.AU
Maas Resumed With A$4.95/Share Target Price by Macquarie>MGH.AU
Timee, Watami Launch Subway Stores Staffed by On-Demand Workers
data.description:
Timee has partnered with Watami's subsidiary to open Subway stores staffed by workers from its on-demand job platform, aiming to tackle labor shortages in Japan's food industry, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The first store, opening Apr. 10 in Yokohama, will operate with Timee workers only. A second site in Shinjuku will launch Apr. 24 with Timee-hired leaders and spot staff.

The alliance may evolve into a pipeline for long-term employment. Watami plans to expand Subway to 3,000 stores nationwide.
